title: Broken even-number check
code:
def is_even(n: int) -> bool:
    return n % 2 == 1

expected_output.explanation: The function returns True for odd numbers because the modulo comparison is reversed. It should compare against 0 for even numbers.

title: Top scores logic bug

expected_output.explanation: The function sorts the scores in ascending order and returns the first three values, so it returns the lowest scores instead of the highest. It should sort descending or select the last three items.

title: Unsafe user lookup

expected_output.explanation: The query is vulnerable to SQL injection because user input is interpolated directly into the SQL string. Use a parameterized query with placeholders instead of string formatting.

title: React stale closure interval

expected_output.explanation: The setInterval closure creates a stale reference to `count` at initial render (0), causing it to repeatedly set the count to `0 + 1`. It should use the functional updater form `setCount(prev => prev + 1)` or add `count` to the dependency array.

title: Go loop variable pointer leak

expected_output.explanation: Prior to Go 1.22, the loop variable `u` is shared across iterations. Taking its address results in `ptrs` containing pointers to the same memory location, meaning it prints the last element repeatedly. The fix is to copy the variable inside the loop: `u := u`.

title: Type-coercion equality bug

expected_output.explanation: The loosely equal operator `==` allows type coercion, meaning `"1" == 1` or `null == undefined` is true, which is a security oversight for authorization logic. Use the strict equality operator `===` instead.

title: Path Traversal vulnerability

expected_output.explanation: The code blindly joins user input `filename` into the file path. An attacker can use `../` to access unauthorized files on the server (Directory/Path Traversal). The filename must be sanitized or verified to reside strictly within the uploads directory.

title: Django ORM N+1 queries
code:
from myapp.models import Author

def get_all_books():
    authors = Author.objects.all()
    return [author.book_set.all() for author in authors]

expected_output.explanation: This causes an N+1 query problem because it fetches all authors in one query, but then issues an additional query for each author's books during the list comprehension. It should use `prefetch_related('book_set')` to mitigate the performance hit.

title: Default submit behavior
code:
<form onsubmit="login()">
  <input type="text" name="user">
  <button class="action-btn" onclick="cancel()">Cancel</button>
  <button class="primary-btn">Login</button>
</form>

expected_output.explanation: Both buttons will submit the form by default because a `<button>` element inside a `<form>` defaults to `type="submit"`. The Cancel button should explicitly have `type="button"`.

title: React layout infinite loop

expected_output.explanation: This creates an infinite render loop. The `useLayoutEffect` depends on `width`, and inside it calls `setWidth`. Since `Math.random` produces a new value every time, it will trigger another layout effect infinitely. The dependency array should be empty or conditional logic must be added.
